faq.question: What is hull speed?
faq.answer: Hull speed is the maximum efficient speed of a displacement hull, determined by waterline length. At this speed, the bow and stern waves merge. Pushing past hull speed requires exponentially more power.

faq.question: Can a boat go faster than hull speed?
faq.answer: Displacement hulls (sailboats, trawlers) are limited near hull speed. Planing hulls (speedboats, sportfish) can break through and ride on top of their bow wave, achieving much higher speeds.

faq.question: Where is hull speed 1.34 from?
faq.answer: The constant 1.34 comes from the relationship between wave speed and wavelength. A wave's speed in knots equals 1.34 times the square root of its wavelength in feet. At hull speed, the boat's bow wave wavelength equals the waterline length.

faq.question: Is the Boat Hull Speed Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard boating and marine formulas trusted by boaters and sailors. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Boat Hull Speed Calculator?
sections.body: The Boat Hull Speed Calculator is a free online tool designed for boaters and sailors who need quick, accurate calculations in the boating and marine space. By entering your waterline length, you get instant results including hull speed, hull speed, hull speed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting hull speed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In boating and marine, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by boaters and sailors worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to navigate safely with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.
